case_name: Tracey King (As Personal Representative of the Estate of Kevin King, Deceased)   v South Tees NHS Hospital Foundation Trust [2020] EWHC 416 (QB) (28 February 2020)

evidence_index.text: On 22 nd May 2016 the deceased attended the accident and emergency department of James Cook University Hospital complaining of a number of symptoms.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The diagnosis made was of a deep vein thrombosis in the left leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The chest x-ray was reported on 26 th May 2016. It identified a 3cm. focal area of increased opacification in the left mid zone.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: No further action was taken, however, following this report.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 2 nd February 2017 Mr Kevin King ("the deceased") died due to lung cancer.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On 25 th November 2016 a further chest x-ray was performed. This showed that the opacity described on the previous image of 22 nd May 2016 had increased significantly in size.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The deceased was urgently referred to a consultation with a respiratory physician who saw him on 6 th December 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A CT scan was performed. On 14 th December 2016 a PET scan was performed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Doctor Peake says there is significant uncertainty regarding the likely staging of the deceased's disease as at June 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Doctor Peake

evidence_index.text: Doctor Lester's opinion was that the deceased had no regional lymph node metastasis (N0).
evidence_index.speaker: Doctor Lester

evidence_index.text: Doctor Peake's opinion is that the deceased had at least metastasis in the ipsilateral mediastinal and/or subcarinal nodes (N2).
evidence_index.speaker: Doctor Peake

evidence_index.text: Breach of duty in this regard is admitted in paragraph 9 of the defence.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The issue to be determined by the court is how far advanced the cancer was in June 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: If Doctor Lester's view is accepted then the oncologists agree that treatment would have been with surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: If Doctor Peake's opinion is correct then the oncologists agree that surgery would not have been offered in June 2016.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Having considered these two points, although they have merit in themselves, they do not in my judgment have any real effect in undermining the quality of Doctor Lester's evidence as a whole.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

reveal.clinical_significance: Supports diagnosis of advanced lung cancer

options.description: Order CT scan for further assessment

Defendant did not act on the x-ray findings

expected_action_court.description: CT scan should have been ordered

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to act on suspicious x-ray findings
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CT scan should have been performed to rule out malignancy

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to order timely CT scan
Causation established - earlier diagnosis could have altered treatment options

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely CT would have confirmed diagnosis earlier
